Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN W. HOWARD, Esq. of JW Hoard/Attorneys, Ltd. (Tel: 619-234-2842) for plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is overruled, with leave to appropriately re-file.

After listing their names, the entirety of defendants' demurrer is: "Plaintiffs lack standing to pursue declaratory and injunctive relief, and [] Plaintiffs fail to state facts to support their Causes of Action." This is inadequate.

The Code of Civil Procedure mandates: "A demurrer shall distinctly specify the grounds upon which any of the objections to the complaint?are taken. Unless it does so, it may be disregarded." (CCP 430.60.) "Each ground of [a] demurrer must be in a separate paragraph and must state whether it applies to the entire complaint or to specified causes of action." (CRC 3.1320(a).) Where, as here, there are there are several defendants, "the demurrer should specify on which defendant's or defendants' behalf each ground is asserted." (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 7:107.)

Any re-filed demurrer shall be supported by a memorandum keyed to each demurrer ground. (Id. at 7:118 ("Each ground for demurrer should be separately stated and supported in the points and authorities.").) The parties are also directed to state in their briefs the elements of each of the SAC's six causes action

Order signed.
